item.title: Device-scaling constraints imposed by the van der Waals gap formed in two-dimensional materials
item.author: Mahdi Pourfath
item.content:encoded:
Transistor miniaturization requires controlling gate leakage through ultrathin dielectrics and minimizing source/drain contact resistance. Although two-dimensional (2D) semiconductors offer excellent electrostatic control, their interfaces with gate ...

Mahdi Pourfath

Science

item.title: Our cosmos, ourselves

item.title: Cold rush
item.author: Zack Savitsky

item.title: Observation of Kardar-Parisi-Zhang universal scaling in two dimensions
item.author: Simon Widmann
item.content:encoded:
Equilibrium and nonequilibrium states of matter can exhibit fundamentally different behavior. A key example is the Kardar-Parisi-Zhang universality class in two spatial dimensions (2D KPZ), where microscopic deviations from equilibrium give rise to ...

Simon Widmann

Science

item.title: Modular enantioselective photocatalysts from privileged pybox scaffolds
item.author: Riley M. Kelch
item.content:encoded:
Modern organic synthesis relies upon the availability of chiral catalysts to control the stereochemistry of bond-forming reactions. Several families of chiral catalysts have become recognized as “privileged” structures because of their notable generality ...

Riley M. Kelch

Science

item.title: How artificial intelligence is reengineering protein engineering
item.author: Jennifer Listgarten
item.content:encoded:
Over the past decades, protein engineering has matured into a field of its own, driven by computational modeling and high-throughput wet lab experiments, with broad application in therapeutics, diagnostics, agriculture, and manufacturing. In recent years,...

Jennifer Listgarten

Science

item.title: Many-body interferometry with semiconductor spins
item.author: D. Jirovec
item.content:encoded:
Quantum simulators enable studies of many-body phenomena, which are intractable with classical hardware. The manipulation of electronic spin states in devices based on semiconductor quantum dots promises precise electrical control and scalability ...

D. Jirovec

Science

item.title: A high-throughput selection system for fast-acting covalent protein drugs
item.author: Qiongxuan Fan
item.content:encoded:
Covalent protein drugs offer therapeutic potential but are limited by slow target engagement and the absence of high-throughput selection platforms. Rapid covalent binding requires coordinated optimization of affinity, stability, and warhead geometry—an ...

Qiongxuan Fan

Science

item.title: Atomic-resolution imaging of gold species at organic liquid-solid interfaces
item.author: Sam Sullivan-Allsop
item.content:encoded:
The structure and dynamics of adsorbed atoms (adatoms) at solid-liquid interfaces determine the performance of advanced catalysts, electrochemical devices, molecular separation technologies, and metal extraction from waste streams. However, in situ ...

Sam Sullivan-Allsop

item.title: Experimental evidence of a liquid-liquid critical point in supercooled water
item.author: Seonju You
item.content:encoded:
The search for the liquid-liquid critical point in supercooled water is challenging owing to rapid crystallization. We studied supercooled water at timescales before ice formation by heating high- and low-density amorphous ices using infrared ultrafast ...

Seonju You

Science

item.title: High-dimensional topological photonic entanglement
item.author: M. Javad Zakeri
item.content:encoded:
The generation and manipulation of high-dimensional quantum states lies at the heart of modern quantum computation. The use of topology to resiliently encode and transport quantum information has been widely investigated in condensed matter and has ...

M. Javad Zakeri
